skill.name: marketing-ideas
skill.description: Helps SaaS and software teams choose practical marketing ideas from a categorized library of 140 tactics.
skill.summary: Generate targeted SaaS marketing ideas by stage, budget, channel, and growth goal.
skill.author: coreyhaines31

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged weak cryptography and reconnaissance patterns, but review found only marketing guidance text in SKILL.md. No executable code, network calls, filesystem access, environment access, prompt injection attempt, or credential handling was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography detections occur in ordinary marketing text and headings, not in code that implements hashing, encryption, signing, or credential storage. The duplicate line 3 hit is the skill description, and the other locations describe Reddit research, media acquisition, screenshots, competitions, and output formatting.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All cited lines are prose in a single Markdown instruction file. No cryptographic API, algorithm implementation, encoded payload, or secret handling appears at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance detections refer to marketing activities such as building useful tools, paid advertising categories, influencer whitelisting, educational courses, and growth stage guidance. They do not instruct the assistant to inspect the host system, enumerate files, collect environment data, or transmit local information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are contextual marketing advice, not system commands or data collection instructions. No evidence of host reconnaissance behavior was found.

content.value_statement: SaaS teams often need growth ideas that match their stage, budget, and audience. This skill turns a large tactic library into focused recommendations with next steps.
actual_capabilities: Suggests 3 to 5 marketing ideas based on product, audience, stage, and resources.
Organizes 140 tactics across SEO, ads, community, email, partnerships, launches, and product-led growth.
Provides implementation guidance for selected tactics, including first steps and expected outcomes.
Adapts recommendations by budget, timeline, team size, and growth stage.
Prompts for missing context before recommending marketing tactics.
Connects related tactics to specialized skills listed in the source file.
limitations: Does not run campaigns, publish content, buy ads, or connect to marketing platforms.
Does not validate market size, keyword volume, or competitor data without user-provided research.
Recommendations are general guidance and need business judgment before execution.
Some tactics may require legal, privacy, or brand review before launch.

use_cases.title: Plan early growth experiments
use_cases.description: Select low-cost tactics for a new SaaS product with limited traffic and a small team.

use_cases.title: Refresh acquisition channels
use_cases.description: Find new campaign ideas when existing SEO, paid, or email channels have slowed.

use_cases.title: Build a quarterly marketing roadmap
use_cases.description: Group tactics by effort, timeline, and expected outcome for planning cycles.

prompt_templates.title: Get simple recommendations
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with basic product context.
prompt_templates.prompt: I need marketing ideas for my SaaS product. The product is [product], the audience is [audience], and our goal is [goal]. Suggest 3 practical ideas.

prompt_templates.title: Match ideas to budget
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose tactics that fit team and spending limits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Recommend marketing tactics for [product] with a [budget] budget, [team size] team, and [timeline]. Explain why each idea fits.

prompt_templates.title: Create an experiment plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Turn selected ideas into measurable tests.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the marketing ideas library to design a 30-day experiment plan for [product]. Include channels, first steps, success metrics, and required resources.

prompt_templates.title: Prioritize a growth roadmap
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit these current marketing efforts: [current efforts]. Recommend a prioritized roadmap using SaaS marketing tactics, with quick wins, medium-term bets, and long-term investments.

output_examples.input: A founder asks for ideas to launch a developer tool with no paid budget.
output_examples.output: The skill recommends open source as marketing, free tools, and community participation. It explains why each fits, how to start, expected outcomes, and resource needs.

output_examples.input: A growth marketer wants new acquisition channels for a mature B2B SaaS product.
output_examples.output: The skill suggests comparison pages, integration marketing, webinars, and LinkedIn audience building. It ties each idea to intent, distribution, and measurable next steps.

output_examples.input: A marketing lead needs a quarterly roadmap for a product-led SaaS company.
output_examples.output: The skill groups tactics into quick wins, medium-term content programs, and long-term brand investments. It also identifies dependencies and likely outcomes.

best_practices: Provide product, audience, stage, budget, timeline, and current channels before asking for recommendations.
Ask for fewer high-fit tactics instead of a long generic list.
Turn each selected idea into an experiment with a metric, owner, and review date.
anti_patterns: Requesting every tactic without explaining the business context.
Choosing tactics only because they are trendy or inexpensive.
Launching campaigns without brand, legal, privacy, or measurement review when needed.

faq.question: What type of products is this skill best for?
faq.answer: It is best for SaaS and software products that need acquisition, activation, retention, or brand-building ideas.

faq.question: Can it create a full marketing strategy?
faq.answer: It can help structure a strategy, but it needs your market context, goals, and constraints.

faq.question: Does it provide paid advertising setup steps?
faq.answer: It can suggest ad channels and campaign concepts, but it does not configure ad accounts or manage spend.

faq.question: Can it help with low-budget marketing?
faq.answer: Yes. The source includes free and low-budget ideas such as content, SEO, community, templates, and free tools.

faq.question: Does it validate whether an idea will work?
faq.answer: No. It helps choose and frame ideas, but results depend on execution, market fit, timing, and measurement.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
